Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add LE Secure Connections and encryption for BLE HID Mac compatibility (#346)

* Add LE Secure Connections and encryption for BLE HID Mac compatibility

Enable LE Secure Connections with SM_AUTHREQ_SECURE_CONNECTION flag and
ENABLE_LE_SECURE_CONNECTIONS config to support macOS BLE HID pairing.
Add ENCRYPTION_KEY_SIZE_16 flag to all HID report characteristics and
descriptors. Register SM event handler for Just Works confirmation.
Fix att_flags() call in GATT database and mrbc_array_new API usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix mrbc_array_new argument from NULL to 0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use named constants instead of magic number for ATT flags bitmask

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove ineffective ENCRYPTION_KEY_SIZE_16 from characteristic declaration properties

The first argument of add_characteristic is truncated to 8 bits
(properties & 0xff) for the GATT characteristic declaration.
ENCRYPTION_KEY_SIZE_16 (0xf000) has no effect there.
The flag remains in value_properties (3rd argument) where att_flags()
correctly processes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): resolve all 4 CI failure categories on Dependabot PRs

Stage 1 — Python:
- ruff --fix: remove 25 unused imports and f-string prefixes
- rename ambiguous variable `l` → `line` in test_e2e_feed_sync.py and
  test_e2e_cert_renewal.py (E741)
- remove unused `chunk` variable in routes/correlation.py (F841)
- ruff format: reformat 28 files to satisfy format check

Stage 2 — SAST (Bandit):
- add `# nosec B608` to schema-qualified SQL in routes/msp.py — these are
  false positives; `schema` is loaded from the DB (trusted), not user input,
  and PostgreSQL requires schema names to be embedded in SQL literals

Stage 1 — Rust (rustfmt):
- create agent/src/collectors/esf/esf_ffi.rs stub so rustfmt can resolve
  the `pub mod esf_ffi` declaration in esf/mod.rs (file was missing)

Stage 3 — Helm Chart Lint:
- fix ci.yml Helm version from "3.14.x" (invalid semver, download 404) to
  "3.16.0" (matches cd.yml)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use absolute callback URL for Google OAuth in production

When behind nginx reverse proxy, Passport resolves the callback URL
relative to localhost:3000 instead of the actual domain. Use BASE_URL
env var to construct the full callback URL.

commit.message:
feat: add server-side reader control (#147)

## Summary

Add remote reader control to the server dashboard so IPICO readers can
be managed without direct network access to the forwarder. Commands are
proxied through the existing forwarder WebSocket connection using a
request/response pattern.

## Changes

**Protocol**: New `WsMessage` variants for
`ReaderControlRequest/Response`, `ReaderInfoUpdate`, and
`ReaderDownloadProgress`; shared `ReaderInfo` types and
`ReaderControlAction` enum with 10 actions. New `ReadMode`,
`ReaderConnectionState`, and `DownloadState` enums replace
stringly-typed fields (wire format unchanged via serde renames).

**Server**: HTTP endpoints under
`/api/v1/forwarders/{id}/readers/{ip}/`, WS loop proxying, in-memory
reader state cache, 2 new SSE event types. Fire-and-forget commands
(`ClearRecords`, `StartDownload`) use a dedicated
`ReaderControlFireAndForget` command variant (no pending entry leak).
`reader_ip` path parameter validated (400 on invalid format). Request
logging via `tracing::debug`. Serialization failures return
`InternalError` (HTTP 500) instead of misleading timeout.
`DashboardEvent::ReaderDownloadProgress` uses `#[serde(flatten)]` to
avoid field duplication.

**Forwarder**: WS handler delegates all 10 actions to existing
`ControlClient` methods. `SyncClock` now spawns as a background task (no
longer blocks the uplink loop for 3-5s). `ReaderInfoUpdated` events look
up actual reader connection state from `SubsystemStatus` instead of
hardcoding "connected". Unsolicited state pushes on changes.

**Dashboard**: Expandable reader detail panels on stream cards with info
grid, controls, download progress tracking, and feedback banners. Shared
UI helpers moved to `shared-ui` package. `ReaderControlHttpResponse`
type matches actual HTTP response shape. Reconnect button disabled when
forwarder is offline. All SSE event handlers wrapped in try-catch for
malformed JSON resilience. TypeScript string literal union types for
`state` and `mode` fields.

## Test Results

All 466+ Rust tests pass (0 failures), server-ui (233 pass), shared-ui
(110 pass). 7 new integration tests in `reader_control_proxy.rs`
covering: happy path, error forwarding, forwarder disconnect,
fire-and-forget 202, state cache cleanup, 404 not connected, 400 invalid
reader_ip.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
debug: add detailed console.log at each webhook step to diagnose email issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract LWW conflict resolution into UseCase interfaces with atomic DB execution (#89)

* Extract LWW conflict resolution from DB adapters into use cases (#84)

Move timestamp-based Last-Write-Wins conflict resolution logic out of 5 RealDb adapters and into the use case layer. Adapters become pure CRUD (upsert/softDelete), while conflict decisions are made by reusable resolveConflictForSave/resolveConflictForDelete functions in the new lib:sync:be module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use more specific guard for lib:sync:be auto-wiring

Change `!path.contains("sync")` to `!path.startsWith(":lib:sync")` to avoid
accidentally matching future modules that happen to contain "sync" in their path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused id property from Syncable interface

The conflict resolution functions only use updatedAt and deletedAt,
so id is unnecessary in the Syncable contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant lib:core:common dependency from lib/sync/be

The convention plugin auto-wires lib:core:be, which already exposes
lib:core:common via api scope transitively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document auto-wiring in skills and docs

Add notes to gradle_plugins.md, project_structure.md, and both skill
files that convention plugins auto-wire most dependencies and new
modules should start with only the plugin applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix LWW conflict resolution transactional atomicity

Move LWW conflict resolution back into driven adapters' transaction
blocks to restore atomic read-check-write, fixing TOCTOU race condition
introduced by the extraction into use cases.

- Convert top-level functions to UseCase interfaces + internal impls
  in lib/sync/be (ResolveConflictForSaveUseCase, ResolveConflictForDeleteUseCase)
- Restore saveX()/deleteX() port signatures returning BackendX?
- Inject use cases into RealXDb adapters, call inside transaction {}
- Revert feature use cases to thin delegation with logging
- Register syncBeModule in BackendModulesAggregate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Split lib:sync:be into api/impl modules

Rename rejected to isRejected in use case impls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move Syncable to model module and inline result types into use case files

Extract Syncable interface into lib:sync:be:model following the project's
model module pattern. Move SaveConflictResult and DeleteConflictResult from
the separate ConflictResolution.kt into their respective use case interface
files, co-locating custom return types with the use cases that produce them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix missing deleteAll import in TestDatabaseCleaner

Pre-existing issue from main where Exposed v1 moved deleteAll() to the
jdbc package, requiring an explicit import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

#fix: 電源断時のデータ消失・generatePdf()ハングを修正

- AC電源断でシャットダウン要求が来たとき、検査進行中（isExamRunning）なら
  handleExamEnd() を呼んでレポートを保存してからシャットダウンするよう修正
  （従来はデータを保存せずループを抜けていた）

- generatePdf() を system() から CreateProcess + WaitForSingleObject に変更:
  - タイムアウト3分: Python スクリプトがハングしても System 全体が止まらない
  - タイムアウト時は TerminateProcess() で強制終了
  - CREATE_NO_WINDOW でコンソールウィンドウを非表示に

fix: extraer texto de thinking blocks de Gemini 3.1 Pro

Gemini 3.1 Pro devuelve content como lista de bloques
[{type:thinking,...}, {type:text,...}] en lugar de string plano.
Filtrar el bloque type=text en contracts_router y query_flow
para evitar que React intente renderizar el objeto completo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pages): check all wrangler config paths and handle refresh tokens

The wrangler config file location varies by version/platform:
- ~/.wrangler/config/default.toml (wrangler v3+)
- ~/.config/.wrangler/config/default.toml (some installations)
- $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.wrangler/config/default.toml

The previous fix only checked one path. Also, an expired oauth_token
with a refresh_token present should still be considered valid since
wrangler auto-refreshes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove default CMD from Dockerfile to force render.yaml dockerCommand

The Dockerfile's default CMD was being used instead of the dockerCommand
specified in render.yaml, causing the scheduler and worker services to
run Uvicorn instead of their proper processes.

Without a default CMD, Render must use the dockerCommand from render.yaml:
- API: uvicorn theundercut.api.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT
- Scheduler: python -m theundercut.scheduler
- Worker: python -m theundercut.worker

commit.message:
Add visual AIOS Pyramid diagram — key product explainer

Proper stepped pyramid (widest at bottom, narrowest at top):
- Layer 1 (100%): Data Layer with SaaS tool logos
- Layer 2 (82%): Agent Architecture with agent badges + MCP
- Layer 3 (64%): Environment (VPS, Local, On-Prem)
- Layer 4 (46%): AI Models (Claude, GPT, Gemini) — swappable

Ownership brackets: "You own these" for L1-L3, "Swappable" for L4.
Bottom-up staggered animation, hover effects, full EN/JP support.

fix: deep pipeline & DB layer fixes — 18 issues across 10 files

CRITICAL:
- C1: Fix key.split(':') truncating trader IDs with colons (aggregate-daily-snapshots Sharpe)
- C2: Don't early-return on v2 error in upsertTraders — continue processing remaining batches
- C3: inline-jobs: insert→upsert with onConflict + error checking (was silently dropping data)
- C4: Remove ROI *100 double-multiplication in trader detail v1 fallback
- C5: Fix truthy checks (row.roi ? → row.roi != null ?) across 6 API routes — 0 values no longer treated as null

HIGH:
- H2: Composite is_stale now checks actual data freshness instead of hardcoded false
- H3: getSimilarTraders + getTraderStats queries now filter by season_id='90D'
- H4: enrichment-db delete-then-insert now checks delete errors before inserting
- H5: aggregate-daily-snapshots: add season_id='90D' filter + fix date boundary (23:59:59→next day 00:00:00)
- H6: copiers sort column → followers (copiers column doesn't exist in v1 table)

MEDIUM:
- M7: getTraderStats snapshot history filters by season_id to avoid mixing 7D/30D/90D ROI
- M8: Composite fallback query now applies 72h freshness filter (matches precomputed)
- Fix profitableWeeks: roi || 0 → roi ?? 0
- Fix followers || 0 → followers ?? null in shared.ts profile upsert

commit.message:
Lisser le drawdown capital sur toute la période (ageObjectif→ageFin)

Avant : deux annuités séparées (hors PER avant 64, tout après 64)
créaient un pic aberrant au lever de pied puis un crash à 64 ans.

Après : une seule annuité lissée qui intègre l'injection du PER
à 64 ans comme apport ponctuel. Revenu constant en pouvoir d'achat.

Formule : A = (P1×(1+r)^n1 + PER64) / (ann1 + pv2)
